channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Biden is considering preemptive pardons for officials and allies before Trump takes office
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden is weighing whether to issue sweeping pardons for officials and allies who the White House fears could be unjustly targeted by President-elect Donald Trump’s administration, a preemptive move that would be a novel and risky use of the president’s extraordinary c
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Dozens of prominent veterans sign onto letter supporting 'outstanding' Hegseth nomination amid controversies
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump's Defense Secretary nominee Pete Hegseth received a boost Friday from more than 60 retired service members of prominence who defended him against attacks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas’ maternal mortality committee faces backlash for not reviewing deaths from first two years post-Dobbs
item.description: Committee members said it was necessary to skip the two years after Texas passed its restrictive abortion laws to offer more timely recommendations.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Eleanor Klibanoff, KPRC 2

item.title: Daniel Penny to be tapped for Congressional Gold Medal by House GOP lawmaker
item.description: Rep. Eli Crane is planning to introduce a resolution to honor a U.S. Marine veteran on trial for killing a homeless man who was described as acting erratic on the subway.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AOC launches bid for top Democratic role on House Oversight Committee
item.description: Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez, D-N.Y., announced she is running for the top Democratic position on the House Oversight Committee next Congress.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The first rioter tried on Jan. 6 charges gets reduced prison sentence after Supreme Court decision
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A Texas man who was the first rioter to go on trial for the Jan. 6, 2021, attack on the U.S. Capitol was resentenced on Friday to nearly seven years in prison after he delivered an angry, profane rant to the judge who agreed to modestly reduce his original sentence.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Newsom visits border, slams Trump on planned deportations, tariffs
item.description: "No state will be more impacted by that, no region will be impacted by that, small businesses that will lose customers, jobs that will be lost, inflationary impacts on every single one of you," said Newsom.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: Biden EPA makes first-ever climate change arrest
item.description: President Biden's Environmental Protection Agency enforcement report reveals increased penalties for polluters, including the first-ever climate-change related arrest.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: National Police Association endorses Trump pick for FBI director
item.description: The National Police Association praised Kash Patel's record of "transparency" and accountability that they said makes him well-positioned to lead the bureau.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Obama, in 1st remarks since election, says 'a line has been crossed' if 'one side' makes certain moves
item.description: Former President Obama said that it crosses a line for one side to seek to permanently entrench their power.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: TikTok's future uncertain after appeals court rejects its bid to overturn possible US ban
item.description: A federal appeals court panel on Friday unanimously upheld a law that could lead to a ban on TikTok in a few short months, handing a resounding defeat to the popular social media platform as it fights for its survival in the U.S. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Elon Musk funded a super PAC comparing Trump’s position on abortion to Ruth Bader Ginsburg’s
item.description: Elon Musk was the sole funder of a super PAC that focused on advertising likening Donald Trump’s stance on abortion to that of late Supreme Court Justice Ruth Bader Ginsburg.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Elon Musk funded a super PAC comparing Trump's position on abortion to Ruth Bader Ginsburg's
item.description: Elon Musk was the sole funder of a super PAC formed less than a month before the election that focused on advertising intended to convince voters that Donald Trump 's stance on abortion was akin to that of late Supreme Court Justice Ruth Bader Ginsburg.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Feds using banks to surveil Americans' financial data without warrants, House Judiciary says
item.description: FIRST ON FOX: Federal law enforcement has been manipulating the Suspicious Activity Report system to gain access to Americans’ financial information without warrants or probable cause, the House Judiciary Committee said Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Boston City Council member is arrested on fraud and theft charges
item.description: A Boston city councilor has been arrested on federal fraud charges, accused of funneling part of an inflated bonus payment to a staffer to whom she was related into her own pockets during an exchange at a City Hall bathroom.
item.dc:creator: Kathy Mccormack, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Speaker Dade Phelan abandons bid for third term amid bruising leadership battle
item.description: Phelan, a Beaumont Republican, had previously insisted he had enough votes to thwart a challenge from the right led by state Rep. David Cook of Mansfield.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Jasper Scherer, James Barragán And Renzo Downey, KPRC 2

item.title: The US government is closing a women's prison and other facilities after years of abuse and decay
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal Bureau of Prisons is permanently closing its “rape club” women’s prison in California and will idle six facilities in a sweeping realignment after years of abuse, decay and mismanagement, The Associated Press has learned.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and MICHAEL BALSAMO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy bring Trump's DOGE to Capitol Hill
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Billionaire Elon Musk and fellow entrepreneur Vivek Ramaswamy spent several hours Thursday swapping ideas with lawmakers about President-elect Donald Trump'sDOGE initiative to dismantle parts of the federal government.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fox News Politics: Defining DOGE
item.description: The Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump transition, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House rejects Democratic efforts to force release of Matt Gaetz ethics report
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The House shut down Democrats' efforts Thursday to release the long-awaited ethics report into former Rep. Matt Gaetz, pushing the fate of any resolution to the yearslong investigation of sexual misconduct allegations into further uncertainty.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: EPA hails 'revitalized' enforcement efforts as Biden administration heads to exit
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Environmental Protection Agency enhanced enforcement efforts this year, doubling financial penalties issued to polluters and issuing the first-ever arrest for a climate change-related crime, the agency said in a report Thursday.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Imprisoned Proud Boys leader balks at answering a prosecutor's questions about Capitol attack
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An imprisoned far-right extremist group leader who was the top target of the federal investigation of the Jan. 6, 2021, riot at the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Shouting match erupts over 9/11 during Secret Service hearing
item.description: Acting Secret Service Director Ronald Rowe and Rep. Pat Fallon (R-Texas) engaged in a shouting match that persisted over gavel banging, as Rowe accused the lawmaker of politicizing the Sept. 11 attack.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Talking Sense: Coming together to solve tough problems
item.description: Mariah Levison and Rob Fersh help people find consensus on how to solve divisive issues, ranging from prison reform to what art should hang in the Minnesota Capitol. Learn how they do it in this MPR News Talking Sense conversation hosted by MPR News correspondent Catharine Richert.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis, Catharine Richert, Maja Beckstrom, and Annie Baxter, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court leans toward upholding Tennessee's restrictions on gender-affirming care
item.description: The Supreme Court’s conservatives appeared to lean toward upholding Tennessee’s ban on gender-affirming care for minors in a blockbuster showdown over transgender rights Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Bitcoin tops $100K on optimism over Trump crypto plans
item.description: It last traded at $100,027 as of 0240 GMT, up 2.2% on the previous session, after earlier rising as high as $100,277.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: California lawmakers kick off special session to 'Trump-proof' the Golden State
item.description: California lawmakers on Monday are kicking off a special legislative session in what Gov. Gavin Newsom (D) has deemed efforts to "Trump-proof" the Golden State from potential federal interference.
item.dc:creator: Sharon Udasin, NewsNation

item.title: Elon Musk's more than $50 billion pay deal at Tesla was rejected again. Here is why
item.description: A judge in Delaware has for the second time struck down a compensation package for Elon Musk after a Tesla shareholder filed suit.
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster, MPR News

item.title: Sen. Omar Fateh announces candidacy for Minneapolis mayor, says Frey is ‘failing’ residents
item.description: The first Somali elected to the Minnesota Senate is now seeking to lead the states largest city. Sen. Omar Fateh announced Monday he will challenge incumbent, and fellow DFLer Jacob Frey for the mayor’s office.
item.dc:creator: Cari Spencer, MPR News

item.title: Canadian Prime Minister Trudeau flies to Florida to meet with Trump after tariffs threat
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau flew to Florida on Friday to have dinner with President-elect Donald Trump at his Mar-a-Lago club after Trump threatened to impose sweeping tariffs on Canadian products.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES and FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: On the Record: NYPD Commissioner Jessica Tisch
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York City has a new police commissioner. Jessica Tisch is now the second woman to lead the nation’s largest police department.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11
